citations.narrative:
The facility is not following the written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC):
-The PVC states to administer a Bordetella vaccine annually for adult dogs, the facility is not administering this vaccine to
adults.
-The PVC states to administer Neopar vaccine for puppies at 4 weeks of age, the facility is not administering this
vaccination.
-The PVC states to administer Distemper and Parvovirus vaccinations annually to adults. For adult female Pug (#1332),
adult female Golden Retriever (#1887), Adult female Miniature Schnauzer (#1411), adult female Corgi (#1548), and adult
female Corgi (#1541), these animals last received the Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines on 12/2/2023. Additionally,
adult male Cavalier King Charles Spaniel (#0548) last received the Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines on 9/6/2024.
-Approximately 20% of puppies receive a Bordetella vaccination at 5 weeks of age, the PVC does not authorize this
vaccine for puppies.
-The PVC states to administer a Neopar vaccine at 4 weeks and a Distemper/Parvovirus vaccine at 6 weeks. A litter of 6
Cavalier King Charles Spaniels (#5131, 5120, 5130, 5134, 5140, and 5141) did not receive their first vaccines until they
were approximately 8 weeks old.
Failure to administer required vaccines in accordance with a veterinary approved schedule may result in animals to not be
adequately protected against disease and/or can cause pain and distress. Correct by ensuring all animals are vaccinated
in accordance with the schedule approved by the Attending Veterinarian and contact the Attending Veterinarian to review
records and create a plan to determine when to administer future vaccinations for the applicable animals.

The facility was unable to provide documentation of fecal tests performed last year. The facility stated that fecal tests for
adults were performed last year in accordance with the schedule in the Program of Veterinary Care but they could not
provide the test results. Failure to document testing results may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if
the animals are receiving adequate veterinary care. Medical records must be maintained and include the dates and
findings/results of all screening, routine, or other required or recommended test or examination. Correct by documenting
the results of all testing and ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “A” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by April 3, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility is not following the written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC): 
 
-The PVC states to administer a Bordetella vaccine annually for adult dogs, the facility is not administering this vaccine to 
adults.  
-The PVC states to administer Neopar vaccine for puppies at 4 weeks of age, the facility is not administering this 
vaccination. 
-The PVC states to administer Distemper and Parvovirus vaccinations annually to adults. For adult female Pug (#1332), 
adult female Golden Retriever (#1887), Adult female Miniature Schnauzer (#1411), adult female Corgi (#1548), and adult 
female Corgi (#1541), these animals last received the Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines on 12/2/2023. Additionally, 
adult male Cavalier King Charles Spaniel (#0548) last received the Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines on 9/6/2024. 
-Approximately 20% of puppies receive a Bordetella vaccination at 5 weeks of age, the PVC does not authorize this 
vaccine for puppies. 
-The PVC states to administer a Neopar vaccine at 4 weeks and a Distemper/Parvovirus vaccine at 6 weeks. A litter of 6 
Cavalier King Charles Spaniels (#5131, 5120, 5130, 5134, 5140, and 5141) did not receive their first vaccines until they 
were approximately 8 weeks old. 
 
Failure to administer required vaccines in accordance with a veterinary approved schedule may result in animals to not be 
adequately protected against disease and/or can cause pain and distress. Correct by ensuring all animals are vaccinated 
in accordance with the schedule approved by the Attending Veterinarian and contact the Attending Veterinarian to review 
records and create a plan to determine when to administer future vaccinations for the applicable animals. 
3.13(b)(4) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility was unable to provide documentation of fecal tests performed last year. The facility stated that fecal tests for 
adults were performed last year in accordance with the schedule in the Program of Veterinary Care but they could not 
provide the test results. Failure to document testing results may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if 
the animals are receiving adequate veterinary care. Medical records must be maintained and include the dates and

findings/results of all screening, routine, or other required or recommended test or examination. Correct by documenting 
the results of all testing and ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “A” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by April 3, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
